message:
fix(connection): qualified column reference binding in GROUP BY/ORDER BY/HAVING with shadowing aliases, collation lookup consolidation

Fix qualified column reference vs SELECT alias binding bug: when a
SELECT clause aliases a column (e.g., SELECT product AS region), a
qualified reference like sales.region in ORDER BY/HAVING/GROUP BY was
incorrectly binding to the alias instead of the actual source column.

Collation lookup consolidation:
- Three separate inline blocks that resolved collation by borrowing
  self.schema and manually iterating table schemas replaced with calls
  to the existing join_expr_effective_collation() helper (newly added
  as a thin wrapper around join_expr_resolved_collation()), eliminating
  ~80 lines of duplicated schema-iteration code and removing a
  now-unnecessary schema_snap clone

ORDER BY resolution deduplication:
- ~30-line inline block for checking whether an ORDER BY term already
  maps to a result column replaced with resolve_order_term_idx()
- New resolve_named_order_term_idx() helper extracted to share
  table-qualifier-aware matching logic between resolve_order_term_idx
  and sort_rows_by_order_terms, eliminating another ~30 lines of
  duplicated matching code

HAVING evaluation fix:
- evaluate_having_value now respects table qualifiers on column
  references, so HAVING sales.region = 'East' correctly resolves to
  the source column rather than a shadowing alias

Six new tests cover: qualified ORDER BY in joins, qualified ORDER BY
and HAVING in GROUP BY, and collation propagation through join aliases
for GROUP BY and MIN aggregates. Two new compatibility test entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
